expectations.text: Response uses resource:action permission pattern (e.g., booking:read)
Response defines role-permission mappings following least-privilege
expectations.evidence: Three roles with progressively restricted permissions: admin gets all 18 permissions, staff gets 7 (booking CRUD + read-only member/room/billing), member gets only 3 (booking:read, booking:create, room:read). This follows least-privilege.

expectations.text: Response includes Drizzle pgTable schemas for roles and permissions
expectations.evidence: Section 4 defines pgTable schemas: "export const roles = pgTable('roles', {...})" and "export const permissions = pgTable('permissions', {...})" with full column definitions.
Both junction tables defined: "export const userRoles = pgTable('user_roles', {...})" and "export const rolePermissions = pgTable('role_permissions', {...})" with composite primary keys.

expectations.text: Response includes foreign keys and indexes
expectations.evidence: Foreign keys: ".references(() => users.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' })", ".references(() => roles.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' })", etc. Indexes: "userIdx: index('user_roles_user_idx').on(table.userId)", "roleIdx: index('user_roles_role_idx').on(table.roleId)", and more.
